name: Crosspoint Matrix
one_liner: transmission.video's signature NxN crosspoint routing grid brought to life: inputs cross outputs, crosspoints ignite as routes activate, and glowing packets race down the lit row-then-column paths to their destinations -- 'Routing Redefined' as living light.
visual_description: A precise, grid-aligned schematic on a near-black engineering stage: a row of input ports down the left edge and a column of output ports across the top, with a faint cool-gray NxN lattice between them -- the crosspoint matrix, exactly the UI transmission.video is known for. As routes activate, individual crosspoint cells IGNITE: a cyan node lights at the intersection, the input row lights up to it and the output column lights down from it, drawing a clean right-angle path. Then glowing packets stream along those lit L-shaped paths -- racing right along the active row, turning at the lit crosspoint, racing up the active column to the output -- quantized dots you can count, in cyan/green/amber for the separated essences. Routes activate and deactivate on a steady PTP beat, so the matrix reconfigures live like a real running router, with a faint 1Hz port-stat counter feel and PTP-lock indicators glowing at the corners. Heavy bloom on the lit crosspoints and packets; the unlit lattice stays disciplined and dim. It reads instantly as a live signal router -- the most direct, on-brand homage to the brand and the most legible 'routing' statement on the floor.
technique: Single self-contained GLSL TOP generator (no input). The shader draws the matrix analytically: a dim grid lattice + input/output port markers, then for a deterministic time-varying set of active routes (input i -> output j chosen by a hashed schedule that cycles on the PTP beat) it lights the crosspoint cell, the row segment from input i to column j, and the column segment from row i to the output. Glowing packets are bright Gaussian dashes whose parametric position advances along the L-shaped (row-then-column) path with time and wraps. Active routes fade in/out on the PTP beat (live reconfiguration). Corner PTP-lock indicators + a faint counter texture. Bounded constant loops over routes. Bloom + scanline/grain finish. No feedback, no input -- drops in anywhere.

network_plan.name: glsl_matrix
network_plan.role: The whole piece. Generator (no input): grid lattice + ports + active crosspoints + lit L-paths + traveling packets + PTP reconfiguration + lock indicators. Generator MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res; NEVER uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Three vec4 uniforms via the vec SEQUENCE (declared manually). Place nodeX 400, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom
network_plan.role: Bloom + finish. Input0 = glsl_matrix. Bright-pass + bounded blur added back so lit crosspoints and packets bloom while the dim lattice stays disciplined; faint scanline + grain. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Place nodeX 800, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: out1
network_plan.role: Manifest output_op / capture target, named out1. Viewer flagged. Place nodeX 1200, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_matrix_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_matrix. See glsl[0]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_matrix_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_matrix.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_bloom. See glsl[1]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_bloom.

network_plan.name: annotate_matrix
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the matrix generator (glsl_matrix). Mode annotate, Opacity 0.3.

params.Bodytext: One generator GLSL TOP draws transmission.video's signature NxN crosspoint grid. A hashed, PTP-beat schedule picks active routes (input i -> output j); for each, the crosspoint cell ignites, the row lights from the input to that column, and the column lights up to the output. Glowing packets then race the L-shaped (row-then-column) path -- right along the row, turn at the lit crosspoint, up the column -- to their destination. Routes reconfigure live on the beat, like a real running router. 'Routing Redefined.'

network_plan.name: annotate_finish
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over bloom + out (glsl_bloom, out1). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: Bloom lights the active crosspoints and packets while the unlit lattice stays disciplined and dim -- the brand's premium engineering restraint. A faint scanline + grain + corner PTP-lock indicators add the live-telemetry feel. Terminates in out1.

exposed_params.name: Gridsize
exposed_params.label: Matrix Size (N)
exposed_params.drives: uGrid.x -- the NxN crosspoint matrix dimension (inputs x outputs). Smaller = a bold readable router; larger = a dense schematic with many ports.

exposed_params.name: Activeroutes
exposed_params.label: Active Routes
exposed_params.drives: uGrid.y -- how many routes are lit simultaneously. More = a busier router with multiple signals flowing at once. Each route runs a constant-bounded packet loop; cap 8.

exposed_params.name: Latticeglow
exposed_params.label: Lattice Glow
exposed_params.drives: uGrid.z -- brightness of the dim unlit grid lattice + ports. Low = the disciplined dark schematic the brand favors; 0 hides the grid entirely.

exposed_params.name: Crosspointglow
exposed_params.label: Crosspoint Glow
exposed_params.drives: uGrid.w -- brightness of the lit crosspoint nodes and their row/column paths. The ignite intensity when a route activates.

exposed_params.name: Packetspeed
exposed_params.label: Packet Speed
exposed_params.drives: uPacket.x and the time term uPacket.w -- how fast packets race the L-path from input to output. 0 freezes them (and drops that time-dependence); higher = near-light-speed.

exposed_params.name: Packetdensity
exposed_params.label: Packet Density
exposed_params.drives: uPacket.y -- how many packets travel each active route at once (1-6). More = a denser data flow along the lit paths.

exposed_params.name: Streams
exposed_params.label: Essence Streams
exposed_params.drives: uPacket.z -- number of separated essence colors assigned to routes (1=cyan video, 2=+green audio, 3=+amber ancillary). The ST2110 separated flows through the router.

exposed_params.name: Ptprate
exposed_params.label: PTP Beat Rate
exposed_params.drives: uClock.x -- frequency of the PTP sync pulse the corner lock indicators beat to. The router's live PTP-lock heartbeat.

exposed_params.name: Reconfigrate
exposed_params.label: Reconfigure Rate
exposed_params.drives: uClock.y -- how often the active route set reconfigures (new input->output assignments fade in/out). The live-router reconfiguration cadence. Lower = routes hold longer; higher = rapid re-routing.

exposed_params.name: Lockindicators
exposed_params.label: Lock Indicators
exposed_params.drives: uClock.z -- brightness of the green PTP-lock indicator dots in the corners. 0 hides them; higher emphasizes the live-telemetry feel.

exposed_params.name: Bloom
exposed_params.label: Bloom
exposed_params.drives: uBloom.x -- intensity of the bloom over lit crosspoints and packets. The booth-scale glow knob; the dim lattice stays disciplined below the bright-pass threshold.

palette.ratio: ~85% dark ground + disciplined cool-gray lattice (the brand's monochrome restraint), ~15% saturated lit routes/packets; cyan leads, green/amber differentiate essences, color appears ONLY as the active signal.

conference_fit: The most direct homage to transmission.video -- their actual signature UI is an interactive NxN crosspoint routing matrix, and the tagline is 'Routing Redefined.' This piece literally enacts routing: inputs cross outputs, crosspoints ignite, packets race the routed L-path to their destination, and the matrix reconfigures live like the real product (sub-second updates, PTP-lock indicators, port-stat feel). It honors the brand's monochrome engineering discipline (dim disciplined lattice, color only as signal) while erupting into controlled signal color -- exactly the brief's strategy. The most legible 'this is what the company does' statement on the floor, on-brand and on-theme at once.

prompt.one_liner: A self-contained crosspoint-routing-matrix generator: an NxN grid where active routes ignite the crosspoint and its row/column path, glowing essence-colored packets race the L-shaped input->output route, and the matrix reconfigures live on a PTP beat with corner lock indicators -- transmission.video's 'Routing Redefined' as living light.
prompt.evolve: Raise Matrix Size for a denser schematic; raise Active Routes + Packet Density for a busier router; raise Packet Speed for fast routing (0 freezes). Lower Reconfigure Rate to hold routes longer (study a single route) or raise it for rapid live re-routing. Lower Lattice Glow for the brand's darkest disciplined look, raise Crosspoint Glow + Bloom for dramatic ignition. Drop Streams to 1 for a single cyan signal. Feed out1 into the kaleidoscope for a routed mandala, or composite the packet-fabric streams ENTERING this matrix. Drive Reconfigure / PTP from real router telemetry (an OSC/WebSocket DAT into the uniforms) for a genuine live-router visualization.

build_notes:
GENERATOR CORRECTNESS: glsl_matrix has NO input -- MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res (res=(1/w,1/h,w,h)); uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator is a compile error. outputresolution MUST be 'custom' + resolutionw/h or it renders 1x1. glsl_bloom HAS an input so sTD2DInputs[0] + uTDOutputInfo.res are valid.

LOOP BOUNDS (GPU crash safety): nested constant-bounded loops -- outer routes (MAXR=8), inner packets per route (k<6), plus the bloom tap loop (R=6). The param counts only early-out via break against the CONSTANT bounds, so no unbounded GPU loop. Cheap: at most 8*6 packet evals + a few segment evals per pixel. GATE PERFORMANCE per performance.md (baseline before, re-check after) but this is comfortably under budget at 1280x720.

UNIFORMS: glslTOP declares uniforms MANUALLY. vec parameter SEQUENCE (op.seq.vec.numBlocks=N, vecNname, vecNtype='vec4', vecNvaluex..w expressions), NOT op.par.vec.

COOK DEMAND: time-dependent via absTime in uPacket.w (packet travel) and uClock.w (PTP + reconfig epoch). Cooks every frame only when out1 demanded; add a temp Execute DAT OUTSIDE the COMP; verify with two captures separated by a real sleep -- AND verify the route set actually RECONFIGURES (the epoch changes on the Reconfigure beat: capture before/after a beat boundary and confirm different crosspoints are lit). Packet Speed=0 freezes packets but uClock.w still references absTime, so the TOP keeps cooking -- correct.

GEOMETRY NOTE: the L-path turn relies on rowY == cp.y; the matrix maps row index i to y via (i+0.5)/N and the crosspoint cp.y is set to the same, so the row->column turn is continuous. Verify in a capture that packets turn cleanly at the crosspoint and do not jump. The toMatrix() margins (0.12/0.10 origin, 0.76/0.80 span) leave room for the input/output ports; confirm the matrix sits centered with port edges visible.

DOCKED DATS: glsl_matrix + glsl_bloom each dock pixel+compute+info; HUG all six under their hosts in the SAME execute_python that creates each op, then get_network_layout. LF/space-indented shader source.

LOOK CHECK: judge by an actual capture (load visual-aesthetics). Verify the lattice reads as a disciplined dim grid (brand restraint), lit crosspoints clearly ignite, the row-then-column L-paths are legible, packets are countable along them, and the reconfiguration reads as a live router (not random flicker). Tune Reconfigure Rate so routes hold long enough to read.

TDN ROUND-TRIP: all 11 params single-component (Int/Float) -> default->value seeding works on re-import (verified v6.0.26). Re-import into a throwaway COMP, confirm a couple .vals + out1 renders, destroy it. operator_count = all ops incl docked DATs (annotations not counted): ~9.
